articles.author: Adam Schreck, Ben Finley, Sam Metz
articles.description: The U.S. military says it fired on Iranian forces and sank six small boats as it moved to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The United Arab Emirates, a key American ally, said it had come under attack from Iran on Monday, the first since a fragile ceasefire took hold in early April. The attacks appeared to be in response to U.S. President Donald Trump’s latest efforts to reopen the strategic strait. The U.S. military said two American-flagged merchant ships had successfully transited the strait on Monday. Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi warned early Tuesday on X that both the U.S. and the UAE “should be wary of being dragged back into quagmire.”
articles.title: US vies with Iran for Strait of Hormuz as UAE comes under attack

articles.author: Chan Ho-him
articles.description: Asian stocks are trading lower, tracking losses on Wall Street after U.S. stocks fell from record highs. Oil prices fell back on Tuesday after jumping the day before as tensions between the U.S. and Iran escalated. U.S. futures edged higher. Markets in Japan, South Korea and mainland China were closed for holidays. The fragile ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran was tested Monday after the U.S. military said it had sank six Iranian small boats targeting civilian ships, while two U.S.-flagged ships successfully passed through the Strait of Hormuz. On Monday, the S&P 500 fell 0.4%, down from its latest all-time high. The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 1.1% and the technology-heavy Nasdaq composite dropped 0.2%.
articles.title: US futures gain and Asian shares slip as oil prices fall back

articles.author: Bassem Mroue
articles.description: A branch of the Catholic church has expressed concern over reports that Israel is demolishing civilian and religious buildings in southern Lebanon. The Council of Melkite Greek Catholic Bishops urged the Lebanese government and the United Nations to protect these properties, especially in Yaroun, where a Melkite convent was reportedly destroyed. Israel has taken control of border areas in its war against Hezbollah, aiming to root out militants. The Israeli military stated it does not intentionally target religious sites. Meanwhile, a potential meeting between Lebanese President Aoun and Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu in Washington is being discussed.
articles.title: Melkite Catholic bishops express concern over Israeli demolition

articles.author: Matthew Lee
articles.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to Rome and Vatican City this week in a bid to ease rising tensions between President Donald Trump and Pope Leo XIV over U.S. policies, particularly the Iran war. The State Department said Monday that Rubio, a Catholic, would be in Italy on Thursday and Friday. The Vatican announced that Rubio would meet with the first American pontiff on Thursday. The trip comes as Trump has criticized Leo for his stances on the Middle East and elsewhere. The pope has said he was not making a direct attack against Trump with his general appeal for peace.
articles.title: Rubio will visit the Vatican as Trump-pope tensions rise

articles.author: Beatrice Dupuy
articles.description: Fashion biggest night is making a statement this year with its dress code, “Fashion is art.” Global Editorial Director of Vogue Anna Wintour and the Metropolitan Museum of Art's Costume Institute curator, Andrew Bolton, are putting their stamp of approval on the idea. Guests from Beyoncé to Nicole Kidman are sure to make an artistic spectacle with the directive. A-list stars and cultural tastemakers are set to take the Met steps Monday as part of the fundraising event for the museum's Costume Institute. For an up-close look at all the red carpet drama, Vogue will host its annual livestream of the night starting at 6 p.m.
articles.title: Nicole Kidman, Naomi Osaka, Anna Wintour arrive at the 2026 Met Gala

articles.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Stars gathered Monday for the 2026 Met Gala, interpreting the dress code “Fashion is art,” inspired by the spring exhibit at the Costume Institute of the Metropolitan Museum of Art.
articles.title: Photos show stars embracing art-inspired fashion at the 2026 Met Gala

articles.description: Rihanna and A$AP Rocky made a joint arrival at the Met Gala, closing out the carpet in signature fashion. Reflecting on their look, Rocky said, “We feel how we look.”
articles.title: A$AP Rocky on the Met Gala carpet: 'We feel how we look'

articles.description: A look at the route guests at the Met Gala take before posing for photographers and walking up the famous steps to enter the Metropolitan Museum of Art. (May 4)
articles.title: Walk in the shoes of a Met Gala attendee

articles.description: Alberta separatists have submitted nearly 302,000 signatures to trigger a referendum on leaving Canada. The group needed 178,000 signatures to force the province to consider the vote. Alberta Premier Danielle Smith has said if the petition is verified, there will be a referendum. She personally does not support the province leaving Canada. On Monday, Mitch Sylvestre, head of Stay Free Alberta, delivered the signatures to Elections Alberta. The petition could face a hurdle this week as an Edmonton judge is expected to rule on a court challenge by Alberta First Nations, who say separation would violate treaty rights.
articles.title: Alberta separatist group says it has enough signatures for referendum on leaving Canada

articles.description: A man spotted carrying a gun near the White House complex was shot by law enforcement officers after he opened fire on them near the Washington Monument. That's according to Secret Service Deputy Director Matt Quinn, who said plainclothes agents spotted the man around 3:30 p.m. Monday in the area near the White House complex and saw the imprint of the weapon on him. The unidentified man attempted to flee when uniformed officers with the Secret Service approached him. Quinn said the man fired at the officers, who returned fire. The alleged gunman was transported to a local hospital, as was a minor who was shot but not seriously injured.
articles.title: Man fires at officers, shot by law enforcement near Washington Monument
